data.title: Peoples Bancorp dividend preview: 10-year growth streak set to extend in April
data.description:
Peoples Bancorp (PEBO) is expected to announce a dividend increase this April, extending its 10-year streak of consecutive dividend growth.

Based on historical trends, analysts expect a consensus annual dividend of ~$1.67 per share, implying a quarterly dividend of ~$0.4175. This would reflect an increase of ~1.8% from the current ~$0.41 per share distribution.

The company last declared a dividend of ~$0.41 per share in January 2026, translating into an annual yield of 4.85%. Earlier, in April last year, it raised the dividend by ~2.5%, from ~$0.40 to ~$0.41.

This financial holding company has delivered a 5-year dividend growth rate of approximately ~3.51% and currently maintains a payout ratio of ~54.85%.

In terms of dividend quality metrics, the company holds ratings of D+ for dividend safety, C- for growth, B+ for yield, and A- for consistency.

Peoples Bancorp (PEBO) is scheduled to report its Q1 2026 earnings on April 21, 2026, before the market opens.

data.title: Dynex sees spreads potentially trading toward 120 bps as it reports $13.31 estimated book value quarter-to-date
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dynex Capital, Inc. (DX) Q1 2026

Management View

  • "We managed the portfolio through a short burst of volatility, which we used to opportunistically raise and deploy capital. We grew the total capital base by 18%, deploying the funds during the quarter as MBS spreads widened. Since quarter end, MBS spreads have tightened and book value is higher." (Co-CEO, President & Director Smriti L. Popenoe)
  • "I'd like to begin by welcoming [ Kaitlyn Mauritz ], who joined Dynex today to lead Capital Markets and Investor Relations." (Chief Financial Officer Michael Sartori)
  • "Book value ended the quarter at $12.60 per share, and economic return was negative 2.5% for the quarter, consisting of $0.51 per share of common dividends and an $0.85 per share decrease in book value." (Chief Financial Officer Sartori)
  • "We ended the quarter with leverage at 8.6x versus total equity" and "our liquidity position remained very strong with $1.3 billion in cash and unencumbered securities at the end of the quarter, representing over 46% of total equity." (Chief Financial Officer Sartori)
  • "We have seen Agency MBS spreads to 7-year interest rate swaps begin to trend tighter again" and "we believe spreads can trade towards 120 again with scope for long-term equilibrium spreads closer to 100 basis points." (Chief Investment Officer Terrence Connelly)

Outlook

  • "As geopolitical events evolve and policymakers refocus on domestic issues like housing, we believe spreads can trade towards 120 again with scope for long-term equilibrium spreads closer to 100 basis points." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)
  • "Last quarter, I noted that we expected net supply to be $200 billion this year. So far in 2026, it appears supply could come in even lower." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)
  • "Proposed changes tied to the Basel III endgame could lower the capital cost banks face to hold mortgages" and "financing costs are declining amid the light regulatory regime." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)
  • The current transcript did not include management EPS or revenue guidance.

Financial Results

  • "Net interest income for the quarter rose from $0.28 per share to $0.40 per share, primarily due to declining financing costs, which fell 33 basis points due to the impact of the Federal Reserve's rate cuts in the fourth quarter." (Chief Financial Officer Sartori)
  • "The majority of the increase was attributable to the growth in our investment portfolio of $6 billion, reflecting the deployment of capital raised during the quarter of $442 million." (Chief Financial Officer Sartori)
  • "With respect to expenses, G&A increased quarter-over-quarter, driven primarily by onetime items" and "we expect overall expenses to normalize in the second quarter with full year expense ratio anticipated to be flat or modestly lower versus year-end as we grow our capital base." (Chief Financial Officer Sartori)

Q&A

  • Bose George, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods, Inc., Research Division: "Can we get an update on book value quarter-to-date?" Co-CEO Popenoe: "As of Friday -- Friday's close, the estimated book value was $13.31 per share, net of the accrued common dividend, and that's up 5.6% versus quarter end."
  • Bose George, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods, Inc., Research Division: "Is that across the curve or like on a specific point on the curve?" Chief Investment Officer Connelly: "Yes, I'm quoting those spreads both against the 7-year swap point."
  • Trevor Cranston, Citizens JMP Securities, LLC, Research Division: "How high you'd be willing to take leverage" given potential tightening and volatility? Chief Investment Officer Connelly: "Our leverage... did increase to 8.6x..." and "we're going to remain very opportunistic" and "very resolute in our view on those spreads moving from down to as much as 100 basis points."
  • Trevor Cranston, Citizens JMP Securities, LLC, Research Division: asked about "spec pools versus TBAs". Chief Investment Officer Connelly: "We want to avoid" TBAs because they are "very, very refinanceable and callable" and said Dynex is "positioning for owning significantly more pools."
  • Jason Weaver, JonesTrading Institutional Services, LLC, Research Division: asked about "phasing of capital deployment". Co-CEO Popenoe: "when we see that type of move, that's a signal to us to go put accretive capital that we're raising to work" and "making sure that the cost of capital is lower than the return on the capital that we're deploying."
  • Jason Weaver, JonesTrading Institutional Services, LLC, Research Division: asked about valuation discipline. Co-CEO Popenoe: "we're always going to want the shares to trade at a premium to book value" and "I think we actually deserve to trade at a significant premium."
  • Ameeta Lobo Nelson, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: asked about swap spreads and hedge mix. Chief Investment Officer Connelly: "we expected things to be in the 60% to 80% of the portfolio hedged with interest rate swaps" and "we were right around 70% on a DV01 basis at quarter end."
  • Ameeta Lobo Nelson, UBS Investment Bank, Research Division: asked whether March widening "test[ed] that backstop thesis" for GSE buying. Chief Investment Officer Connelly: "they have proven to be very value based" and "overall, it is playing out roughly as we expected."
  • Merrill Ross, Compass Point: asked about swap tenors and curve views. Chief Investment Officer Connelly: "we don't predict, we prepare" and said the swap book "shorten[ed] up a little bit in that 3- to 5-year tenor" while emphasizing "our highest conviction is that mortgage yield spread is what we're here to earn."
  • Eric Hagen, BTIG, LLC, Research Division: asked if anything "changed" in willingness to raise capital. Co-CEO Popenoe: "I don't think the principles have changed" and "the raising and deploying don't necessarily have to be simultaneous in nature."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was neutral to slightly positive, focused on near-term book value, leverage capacity, hedge mix, and the durability of the GSE bid, including "Can we get an update on book value quarter-to-date?" (Bose George, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods, Inc., Research Division) and "how high you'd be willing to take leverage" (Trevor Cranston, Citizens JMP Securities, LLC, Research Division).
  • Management tone was slightly positive and opportunity-focused, emphasizing preparedness and regime change, including "we don't predict, we prepare" and "we are on the verge of a significant regime change." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)
  • Versus last quarter’s emphasis on strong realized returns, this quarter’s tone centered on volatility navigation and positioning, including "we managed the portfolio through a short burst of volatility" (Co-CEO Popenoe) compared with "The total economic return in the fourth quarter was 10.2%" (COO, CFO & Secretary Robert Colligan).

Quarter-over-quarter Comparison

  • Financial performance shifted from "The total economic return in the fourth quarter was 10.2%" (COO, CFO & Secretary Colligan) to "economic return was negative 2.5%" (Chief Financial Officer Sartori).
  • Balance sheet and liquidity shifted to higher leverage and lower liquidity ratio, from "leverage of 7.3x total equity" and "$1.4 billion in cash and unencumbered securities... over 55% of total equity" (COO, CFO & Secretary Colligan) to "leverage... 8.6x" and "$1.3 billion... over 46% of total equity" (Chief Financial Officer Sartori).
  • Strategy continuity remained centered on policy-driven MBS spread tightening, moving from "it will likely reset the spread regime tighter" (Chief Investment Officer Connelly, prior quarter) to a more specific range: "spreads can trade towards 120... closer to 100 basis points." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly, current quarter)
  • Management roles evolved: last quarter featured "we separated the roles of Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer" (Co-CEO Popenoe), while this quarter highlighted "[ Kaitlyn Mauritz ]... to lead Capital Markets and Investor Relations." (Chief Financial Officer Sartori)

Risks and Concerns

  • "Global events, most notably the war in Iran, shifted market focus toward geopolitics and drove a sharp increase in volatility." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)
  • "Agency mortgages traded like much riskier assets" during volatility, which management framed as both a risk and an entry point: "we navigated that volatility constructively and selectively added assets as spreads widened." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)
  • Prepayment and convexity management remained central, with technology highlighted as a driver: "technology-driven" heterogeneity in prepayment behavior is creating "meaningful dispersion across pools." (Chief Investment Officer Connelly)

Final Takeaway

Management described Q1 as a quarter of volatility-driven repositioning: capital was raised and deployed as spreads widened, leverage increased to 8.6x, and book value ended at $12.60 per share, while quarter-to-date estimated book value was cited at $13.31 per share (net of the accrued dividend). Executives repeatedly tied the opportunity set to policy support for housing finance, expectations for tighter MBS spreads over time, and ongoing portfolio shifts away from more callable TBA exposure toward specified pools.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AeroVironment wins $14.6M U.S. Army drone contract
data.description:
AeroVironment (AVAV) said it received a $14.6 million production contract from the U.S. Army for its VAPOR Compact Long Endurance unmanned aircraft system, adding to the defense contractor’s portfolio of tactical drone programs.

The award covers the company’s all-electric vertical takeoff and landing, or VTOL, aircraft under the Army’s Company-Level Directed Requirement Small Uncrewed Aircraft System program, Tranche 2, according to a company statement released Monday.

The contract is tied to the Army’s Medium Range Reconnaissance initiative, one of several procurement efforts aimed at expanding battlefield drone capabilities.

For investors, the award reflects sustained Pentagon demand for small unmanned systems used in reconnaissance, surveillance and target acquisition, an area that has drawn increased funding as the military adapts lessons from conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East. It also adds incremental backlog for AeroVironment (AVAV), whose growth has been increasingly tied to loitering munitions and autonomous systems.

The Army’s Company-Level Directed Requirement program is designed to provide Brigade Combat Teams with commercially available drone systems that can be deployed quickly to meet near-term operational needs.

AeroVironment said the VAPOR CLE platform is built for operations in difficult environments including cold-weather terrain and maritime settings. The system uses a modular design that allows multiple sensor payloads and other mission configurations.

The company said the aircraft can be assembled and launched in about two minutes from a compact transport case. It is capable of carrying payloads of as much as 24 pounds (10.89 kilogram), including sensors or munitions, with up to two hours of endurance.

Financial details beyond the announced contract value were not disclosed, and the company did not specify a delivery timeline.

AeroVironment has expanded beyond its legacy small-drone business in recent years through acquisitions and growth in autonomous systems, but U.S. military demand for tactical unmanned aircraft remains a core revenue driver.

data.title: Fifth Third Bank maintains cautious approach to software and data center lending
data.description:
Fifth Third Bank (FITB) is holding firm on its conservative lending strategy for the software and data center sectors, even as artificial intelligence infrastructure demand continues to surge across the financial industry.

During the bank’s conference call on Friday, executives outlined their deliberately restrained approach to this high-growth market segment. The bank acknowledged the promising long-term outlook for AI infrastructure while simultaneously flagging concerns about market volatility.

“On software and data center lending, we have maintained that same disciplined posture,” the bank stated during the call. “We believe in the long demand for AI infrastructure, but we have also seen how quickly these build cycles can overshoot.”

The financial institution emphasized its selective lending criteria, noting that its exposure remains “intentionally limited.”

Software-related loans currently account for less than 1% of Fifth Third’s total loan portfolio, a figure the bank appears comfortable maintaining.

Despite the restricted exposure, the portfolio continues to perform according to expectations. The bank reported no material migration in credit quality during the quarter, suggesting the conservative strategy is delivering stable returns without elevated risk.

The approach reflects a measured stance toward the AI infrastructure buildout, balancing opportunity with prudent risk management as the sector experiences rapid expansion cycles.

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

Software ETFs: (IGV), (IGPT), (XSW), (AOTS)

data.title: UnitedHealth Q1 Earnings Preview: Optimism over long-term tailwinds
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is set to report Q1 results before market opens Tuesday, with analysts expecting an 8% Y/Y decline in earnings on largely flat revenue.

Wall Street expects EPS of $6.61, down 8.2% year over year, on revenue of $109.66B, up 0.1%.

Over the past two years, UNH has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and topped revenue expectations in 63% of quarters.

However, sentiment has softened heading into the print. Over the last three months, analysts have cut estimates more often than raised them, with EPS seeing 10 downward revisions versus 8 upward, while revenue forecasts logged 10 downward revisions against just 2 upward.

Investors will likely focus on medical cost trends, performance at Optum, membership growth, and management’s full-year outlook.

Analysts and investors will also be tuned in to see hear how UnitedHealth will benefit from the announced 2.48% bump in average Medicare Advantage payments in 2027.

The announcement earlier this month gave an immediate boost to UnitedHealth and other insurers' shares, but analysts remain on the fence over whether this can sustain a long-term rally amid persistent headwinds from medical cost inflation and elevated utilization.

SA contributors were largely positive about the stock, with Mark Bern, CFA, viewing the recent pullback as a compelling long-term entry point. Oakoff Investments also sees recent CMS rate increases and Optum Health restructuring support as bullish for UnitedHealth’s outlook, with incremental revenue and operating profit potential not fully priced in.

On the other hand, SA contributor Far Horizon maintains a Hold, citing ongoing margin pressure from rising medical costs outpacing reimbursement rates, as well as headwinds in the Optum segment, including regulatory scrutiny and margin compression.

The stock, down about 2% YTD, carries a Buy rating from Wall Street, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains cautious at Hold.

data.title: Rivian Illinois factory damaged by tornado
data.description:
A Rivian (RIVN) factory that was being used for parts, storage, and logistics for the R2 SUV was damaged by tornadoes over the weekend, potentially disrupting the company’s upcoming launch of its mid-sized SUV.

While its assembly line continues to operate as usual, the areas damaged by the storm—including the roof and at least one wall of the building—are expected to resume operations this week.

According to the National Weather Service, Rivian’s (RIVN) Illinois factory was impacted by a “significant tornado outbreak” across the upper Midwest on Friday.

Rivian (RIVN) is hoping the R2 will put the company in the black, with the vehicle to be priced competitively to the Tesla (TSLA) Model Y, Hyundai (HYMTF) Ioniq 5, and Ford (F) Mustang Mach-E.

The two-row, five-seat SUV is built on a new structural battery pack with cylindrical cells and acceleration that can take the vehicle from zero to 60 mph in under three seconds. This leads the company to assume an ambitious delivery goal in the “mid tens of thousands.”

The first deliveries are expected in June.

data.title: Honeywell to supply petrochemical technology for Dangote Nigeria expansion
data.description:
Honeywell International (HON) on Monday said it will provide petrochemical processing technologies and catalysts to Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals in Nigeria, part of an expansion aimed at increasing local production of plastics inputs and detergent ingredients.

The agreement centers on Dangote’s refinery and petrochemical complex in Lekki, near Lagos, where the company is building out downstream manufacturing capacity tied to its large refining operation.

The deal underscores a broader trend in emerging markets where large energy producers are seeking to move further into higher-margin petrochemicals rather than exporting raw fuels or relying on imported manufactured inputs.

For investors, the project highlights continued demand for Honeywell’s (HON) industrial process licensing and catalyst businesses, while also signaling Dangote’s push to diversify revenue streams beyond fuel refining as Nigeria and West Africa seek greater industrial self-sufficiency.

Under the arrangement, Dangote will use Honeywell (HON) UOP’s Oleflex technology to produce an additional 750,000 metric tons of propylene annually. Propylene is a key feedstock used in packaging, consumer goods and industrial materials.

Dangote also plans to deploy other Honeywell (HON) UOP technologies and catalysts to produce 400,000 metric tons per year of linear alkylbenzene, or LAB, a chemical commonly used in detergents and cleaning products. Honeywell said the Lekki LAB facility would rank among the world’s largest once fully operational.

Financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed.

Dangote President Aliko Dangote said the project is intended to support Nigeria’s industrial base and reduce import dependence.

Honeywell said it has worked with Dangote on the development of the Nigerian refinery through process technologies, automation systems and engineering support.

The companies said Dangote is using Honeywell (HON) technology as part of plans to expand refining capacity from 650,000 barrels per day to 1.4 million barrels a day by 2028. If achieved, that would place the site among the world’s largest refining complexes.

For Honeywell (HON), the deal adds to its portfolio of long-cycle industrial contracts tied to energy infrastructure modernization and petrochemical capacity growth in developing markets.

data.title: Inspired Entertainment launches its premium iGaming portfolio in South Africa
data.description:
Inspired Entertainment (INSE) announced the launch of its premium iGaming portfolio in South Africa. The company noted that it is starting off with six customers, including Hollywood Bets and Betway, which together account for a significant proportion of the online gaming market according to industry estimates. Notably, the South African market is forecast to double in size by 2030.

The launch in South Africa was enabled through Inspired Entertainment's (INSE) ongoing agreement with Light & Wonder (LNW) and distributed via the Light & Wonder iGaming content marketplace.

Inspired Entertainment said it continues to grow its global interactive footprint across regulated markets, with South Africa representing a key addition to its expanding international presence, a market that is predicted to double in size by 2030. The launch was said to reflect the company's ongoing focus on delivering innovative, engaging, and high-quality gaming experiences to players worldwide.

"Launching our iGaming portfolio with such well-known South African operators, collectively representing the majority of the market, marks an important milestone in our expansion. Providing access to approximately 90% of the market, we are excited to collaborate with these well-known leaders to deliver fresh, engaging experiences to players, including localized features tailored specifically to South African audiences. We look forward to demonstrating how our content performs in this dynamic market," highlighted CEO Brooks Pierce.

data.title: Eni, Repsol plan to start exporting Venezuelan gas by 2031 - Bloomberg
data.description:
Italy's Eni (E) and Spain's Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) reached a deal with the Venezuelan government to start exporting natural gas from the country by the end of 2031, Bloomberg reported Monday.

The agreement allows the companies to more than double production at the massive Perla offshore field and export the fuel as liquefied natural gas from a floating terminal, according to the report.

The Perla field, which Eni (E) and Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) discovered in 2009 and lies in shallow waters near Venezuela's border with Colombia, holds an estimated 17 Tcf of gas, making it one of the largest natural gas discoveries in Latin America.

The field currently produces 585M cf/day of gas, supplying power plants, petrochemical facilities, factories, and homes in western Venezuela; according to the report, the new agreement with the government allows the companies to start exporting once they are supplying 645M cf/day for domestic use.

Eni (E) and Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) also received assurances that the companies will be compensated for billions of dollars worth of gas they have pumped for Venezuela at the field over the years but were not compensated, the report said.

The companies, which plan to install two more platforms in the field by 2028 and start exporting once they reach 1.2B cf/day of production, expect to submit a final development plan to state-owned PDVSA by June, according to the report.

data.title: Micron alone accounts for 51% of all S&P 500 EPS revisions since the war began: Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) alone is responsible for 51% of the entire S&P 500's EPS revision since the war began, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

MU's consensus EPS growth estimate for 2026 stands at a staggering 605%, with its EPS revision since February 27 coming in at 93%, nearly doubling in just weeks as the semiconductor demand surge tied to AI infrastructure and defense tech spending filtered through analyst models.

Goldman Sachs had already flagged Micron as a standout ahead of its earnings, raising its own 2026 EPS estimates 19% above consensus.

Outside of Micron and Broadcom (AVGO) in Info Tech, the remaining top contributors are almost entirely energy companies, reflecting how the conflict has upended oil and gas earnings expectations.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) together contributed 24% of the index's EPS revision, with revisions of 44% and 67% respectively since February 27.

Occidental Petroleum (OXY) posted the most dramatic revision of any energy name at 251%, albeit contributing just 2% to the index-level revision due to its smaller weight. ConocoPhillips (COP), Valero Energy (VLO), and EOG Resources (EOG) also featured prominently, each contributing 3–5% of the total S&P 500 revision.
